concerns.text: T023 (Load aligned_dataset.csv) is marked [P] (parallel-safe). However, T023 is a consumer of the artifact produced by T019 (Generate aligned_dataset.csv) in Phase 3. Marking T023 as [P] implies it can run in parallel with T019 or other US1 tasks, which violates the producer-before-consumer dependency. The [P] tag is incorrect here; T023 must wait for T019 completion.

T034 (Compute SHAP values) is marked [P]. This task consumes the model artifact produced by T026 (Save best XGBoost model) in Phase 4. Marking T034 as [P] suggests it can run in parallel with T026 or other US2 tasks, violating the strict dependency that the model must exist before SHAP analysis can occur. The [P] tag is invalid for T034.

T027 (Compute absolute errors) and T028 (Shapiro-Wilk test) are marked [P]. These tasks depend on the predictions generated by the Linear Baseline (T024) and XGBoost (T025) models. While T027/T028 might be parallel to each other, marking them [P] without explicit exclusion of the training tasks (T024/T025) creates ambiguity. More critically, T027/T028 consume the output of T024/T025; if [P] implies independence from the training phase, this is a violation. The tasks should not be marked [P] if they are grouped with the training tasks in a way that suggests they can start before training finishes.

T011 (Download) and T012 (Verify checksums) are marked [P]. While they are in the same file, T012 logically depends on T011 completing to have a file to verify. Marking them [P] implies they can run simultaneously, which is impossible. They should be sequential, or T012 should not be marked [P] if the tag implies 'can run in parallel with other tasks' but the internal logic is sequential. Better: Remove [P] from T012 or split into distinct parallelizable units if possible (e.g., download multiple files in parallel, then verify all).
